transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
you.
Good afternoon.
No afternoon, sorry.
It's all good.
Did you get any snow where you're at?
No, we got a little bit of a, I wouldn't call it snow, just white stuff on the roof pretty much.
Oh, where are you?
Louisiana.
Oh, I'm in Texas.
Yeah, we got that.
Not like last year where we had like four or five inches and shut the city down for a week.
Oh, really?
Yeah, it was, I grew up in St.
Louis, so like I'm used to it, but Lafayette, Louisiana was not prepared for a couple inches of snow.
No, we're like that in Texas.
I mean, we also have a collective trauma from like a 2020 freeze where the grid like collapsed.
There was no heating for like a week on like a good 20, 30% of the state, think.
And that, of course, like people died, so everyone freaks out.
So, um, but it takes...
We take it pretty seriously.
Like this time it was just ice on the roads everywhere.
And we do not have salt.
Like we don't have snow tires.
don't know.
So they were all the government officials were like, do not leave your house and please.
Yeah.
So we tried our best, but it's, it's cool seeing a little bit of snow in Texas.
Cause I did not grow up around snow, you know, my kids loved it.
I mean, again, I grew up with it, but it was all new to them.
So they were very happy with it.
Oh, even just that thin half an inch of snow.
Yeah.
Well, last year, last year was last year.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Make snowballs, stuff like that.
Yeah.
That's pretty cool.
Yeah.
We didn't, ours was also packed.
I mean, it was like not really difficult.
The dog liked it though.
Pretty clean surface for her, you know?
Hi, Colin.
Hi there.
All right.
I guess we can just dive in.
There's mostly just updates today.
Um,
Um, so we can just go through them one-on-one on like what's in flight.
The first one is scheduling QBR.
Next week doesn't work for you, right, Colin?
Or do you want to keep that?
I got, I got the timing wrong.
Next week works.
And we'll, we'll be RKO the week after.
Okay.
Um, the same time works that like, it's like this block, but plus 30 minutes.
This block plus 30 minutes.
Yeah, it works.
Oh, cool.
All right.
And then any word from Rachel?
She's not on, oh, she's on the invite actually, but we could email her and ask her if she wants to be there.
Like Jason would be there.
It's just that long-term kind of like where we want to go and a few opportunities we see in one of our current streams.
I, she gave it a no, but I will ask her and see if she wants to.
Um, all right.
So we'll keep, that's good.
That's done.
And then, then changing this call, is this good for you or do you want to move it?
I think this will be fine going forward.
moved other things around.
great.
And then code components, POC, this is obviously, you know, we're just waiting on the contracting, but that's not a blocker for y'all to read through the doc or clump it together.
I think it's pretty extensive about, like, exactly what we'd be delivering.
So if y'all want to take some time to add feedback to this, if you want to share this with the PM you mentioned before, that'd be great.

And then on our end, I'm just working on getting all the resourcing set up for this so that we don't waste any time once we actually do kick off the POC, especially if we're, like, very contained to those eight weeks.
Sure.
Yeah.
Cool.
And then last week we talked about the API for icons.
If I can't get this, it's not the end of the world, but I don't know if that's possible.
Yeah, I've been asking a bunch of people on our end.
I don't know if that's possible.
I don't
I'd be able to hopefully get back to you today with one.
Yeah, and it's okay.
mean, we're doing a lot of housekeeping on the integrations work stream right now.
So it's not the end of the world, but obviously it would make our lives a bit easier.
Yeah.
And then, yeah, still just waiting on like if we can reorder the categories and integrations, if we get the green light on that.
We've just been working with how they are currently.
And then I'm still working on the partner communication pipeline for you, Zach.
It's tricky, especially as we're like finding more and more sunset tools.
But we're getting through it.
Integrations, like I mentioned, just a bit of housekeeping on how we're doing that.
We did have a little bit of a capacity dip in our publishing.
So now I've like resourced the account again to supercharge our publishing on that.
And so we should have more information on that as we go.
Integration use case guides, we're at scale now.
So there's 16 live.
I did create a client view for you also.
You can access Airtable and pull out all these links if you ever want to take a look at them.
I'm specific.

I think with this one in terms of optimization, which we can talk more extensively about next week in the QBR too, but like initially we were thinking going more of an SEO approach, but the more we went down that rabbit hole, the more it looked like we would just be competing with ourselves.

And so I think taking a principles first perspective, just like they're doing okay, but thinking more about the user specifically and what's the most useful content for them and optimizing for that is probably where we're headed.
And we're doing a little bit of that work already, but I think there's a little bit of room to continue to build that out.
And I also think that's what Google is like rewarding.
So a bit of a bet to start doing that now and then see where that takes us.
But that's kind of it in terms of updates for today.
I don't know if y'all have any questions.

I think the biggest thing, of course, it's like any feedback y'all have on the one pager and then getting our proposals and, you know, looking back at the last few months of the relationship for the QBR.
I think it's worth our time today if we, I mean, we have the time to talk about what we want to actually, what's planned for the QBR and what we want to talk about in that domain.
Okay.
Let me.
I know it's last minute.
No, no, I have notes on this.
have my outline, but I just, it's an internal doc.
Um, okay.
It's very messy.
So I'm hesitant to share it, but, um, okay.
So primarily we have our three work streams, like two built, one building, uh, industry context.
And you can give me feedback, but what.
What we're looking for and what we are building is, of course, our proprietary tool, which is Check That for AO Visibility.
That's launching next week.
And so we've added Webflows to a workspace on here, but we have not taken as much time building out our custom prompts.

We have access to about 90 days of data for the industry, but because of the way we're approaching these work streams and this content, it's a little bit like, I'm not sure what exactly we'll get back from those prompts and certainly might not relate to conversions.

And so there's an opportunity for us if we want to explore that journey a bit more deeply, especially around like how we answer that beginner developer, like whatever, I think you call it the dangerous dev kind of content in our AO answers.
But we have not really done a lot of loading on that to date.
And that's certainly a direction we can move on.
Like we can talk about those opportunities.
And then also thinking retrospectively, we can just talk about performance, which I think is a bit more of a simple conversation, but that's one opportunity.
And then I have another one we can talk about, but I'd like to hear feedback on AEO specifically before I continue.
I think AEO specifically is a major part that we would all hear about.
Everyone's trying to get their opinion of it.
So understanding how we can be better performant, how we can enter that space is very interesting.

I mean, part of our journey on this is the integration pages, while there's a theory that that's AEO-centric, but I'd love to see how we can possibly approach having better reporting on it tracking better details.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And I, I mean, giving me a week is a bit more time to fill that out.
So this is, this is what the tool looks like.
This is not like publicly available and it's still.
Very much being developed.

It's a little bit buggy right now, but, and I would not pay too much credence to what we have here, but of course, tracking position, sentiment, and citation, branded versus non-branded, which is very important to more of like the conversion stories, but we're kind of looking at a different story here.
So thinking more about like the users kind of interaction with the content, I guess, and how useful that is a little bit harder to map, but not impossible.
And so these are the prompts that we have put in here.

They need to be built out a bit, and we're hoping to integrate this with our content OS, which will help like map to the pages specifically that are being cited versus named, which is always like that kind of tension.
And how much do we care in the Webflow use case about like citation versus actual name visibility is a pretty big one.
And so just kind of, yeah, I can build this out, have a better kind of idea of what this opportunity looks like next week.
And I think yesterday we were testing some like AI overviews of what's.

Webflow comes back on, it seems to like come back a lot in that kind of beginner developer content and a lot less than just like developer content or someone that's already assumed to have that experience.
But yeah, I can like, I'll need a little more time to think through that though.
and then the other opportunity, which I'm don't want to like scare anyone with this is the option of doing programmatic SEO.

So I think as we continue, this is like a pitch we put together for programmatic, which I'll also put into the QBR, but thinking through like how useful use case guides are or how much we want to continue to invest in integration pages.
There's like a lot, if this is an opportunity that's interesting to y'all, then we can talk about like how that works out capacity wise.
But the way that we looked at this and we do a lot of programmatic pages.
For a lot of our other clients, like Ramp, and those always perform really, really well.
It's a little different with Webflow because we want to speak highly of both tools that we're comparing.

So for a template, this is what it would look like, and we're thinking more about what the use case is, going over both of the tools for the Webflow integration, thinking about a recommendation for what would be the best tool for whatever your use case is without ever really shitting on the other tool.
And then going through and seeing, this is more of an SEO play too, of course, but this is another option that we could do.
And once we build out this template, could be pretty plug and play, but that's just something else.
Now, was that the original scope that we had?
No, no, no.
This is like an option.
This is an opportunity.
This is something new.
Just looking at like a bit of the overlap between use case guides and integrations, there's like an option to take a little bit more of an SEO.
SEO play that I think this is much more narrow and targeted than what we currently have the potential to do on use case guides.
I'm not suggesting we like abandon use case guides, but it's something to explore.
I think it's really interesting to talk about, especially in more detail at the QBR.
But yeah, I mean, so the integration guides had a very specific use case.
If we're talking about just general marketing blog, that spins back to marketing involvement, which the marketing team has been a little bit distant from us on these builds.
But I think it's worth taking the proposal and talking about it.
And again, remind me that our first initial run on the original Star Wars contract, I know you weren't here for that, but in the original Star Wars contract, we were looking at having...

A programmatic, well, I've called a programmatic, a AI-generated developer blog that would have talked about a bunch of developer topics, not from the perspective of deeply educational, but primarily for SEO and growing a developer adoption.
Is, we started down that pathway and we got a little, we ran around for a while trying to fine tune what we would consider a good blog.
And that's why we split and started work on integrations directly.

Where I would love to go from this conversation is, what has changed since we've, this was a marketing run initiative at first, and then we pulled into it and said, well, it's actually an interesting one because can go technically deep.
I'd love to understand at the QBR more, what has changed since then?
Can we actually deliver blogs that would pass?
What's our marketing content team's sniff test?
What has improved in terms of the modeling for that?
Because truth be told, I would absolutely value being able to have an ongoing series of developer blogs that are just for SEO purposes.
But I think there has been conversation there about why we stopped before was a marketing consideration.
And I want to make sure that we are talking about what has improved since then.
And we can go through old history, if you'd like, on there's a bunch of feedback from Luke Stahl on what was lacking in those blogs.
Okay.
I think I could find those again, unless you have them in your Notion already.
I can pull that.
I'll pull all the call transcripts.
I've been training GPTs on my accounts.
And so I can do that and get all that context for us, figure out what happened, and then answer those questions for sure.
Cool.
Because, yeah, it does precede me a little bit, but I'll figure that out.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That'd be really interesting.
Especially if it got better, I would be very interested in having that type of content.
Absolutely.
Okay.

I want to say, besides, like, looking back at what we've done, then going over, obviously, code components is a given, so it would be meaningful to have any feedback from y'all by then, if you do have any.
And then, let me see, if there's anything else I have on my list.
Yeah.

And then, okay, the go-forward strategy and then providing that context and where we want to fill in those gaps or where we've changed and what value that has or hasn't added is also my to-do list for the QBR.
Is there anything else you'd like me to think about, Colin, or you're curious?
Oh, yeah, probably, but I'm, we have the three streams.
I'd love to, part of the programmatic work, I guess, would be the conversation about...
So what else can we do?
What else should we consider doing that is right in your wheelhouse that you think you could see immediate opportunity for?

And that's going to be a lot of this because there is a finite path where the integration pages are not, I mean, we're not cranking them out like we used to because we're just running out of integration to talk about.
So there is an opportunity to talk about where do we pivot our ongoing efforts to and for expansion.
So I'm definitely on board with that.
We can give feedback this week on the one pager from Kirkland.
Those are our two major streams.
And I guess the other stream is then, I mean, it should be on us.
If I was thinking about truly what is a good QBR, it's on us to help explain how we're measuring success.
And...
And...
I have not been able to get to that point in a meaningful way yet, but maybe I can dig in more this week and try to find some ways to share different ways the marketing team at least views success.
Because what we're trying to view as success is a halo effect of our other, does this, does viewing this increase developer activity in other parts of the funnel?
And that's a really hard correlation to draw, but yeah.
So the TLDR there is I, on our side, we would owe you a reasonable way of how we set goals and metrics and evaluate if we're doing a good job or not.
And I'd love to ask about your AO analysis because a lot of the traffic that we are seeing on these integration pages would be viewed as potentially bot-like.
And so I'm trying to understand, is that good bots or is that bad bots?
Right.
Okay.
Is there any, do you
have any ways to do that measurement at this point?
Sort of.
I mean, I'm working on this with augment code pretty extensively, like testing that process, and the delta was not great.
That came back in terms of like what we needed to update, but that's much more like a very different goal and metric we're working towards.
But we do have a process to work through how we're prompting and how we audit them and then think through where we're going and where that traffic is coming from.
But, yeah, I'll ask, I'll put in a ticket and see that the team can prioritize that so we can take a look at what comes back.
And then with regard to like metrics on y'all's end, that would be super helpful.
I can speak for the whole team working on Webflow right now that they're definitely craving that, like what we're working towards specifically.
And it's coming up a lot.
And so that would be really meaningful to us.
And yeah, but yeah, OK, I got some next steps.
Those are next steps.
Those are next steps.
And I appreciate.
I appreciate everyone going through this so far because it is a little aimless.
So I'm trying to get that together.
It'll be a good QBR conversation.
We'll try to come to the table with a lot of data in order to have better conversations.
Okay, great.
All right.
Well, thank you very much.
And then we'll communicate async.

But if anything comes up in the meantime around feedback on code components or metrics you want us to also be looking at, if we have the ability to look at it, just let me know and I can try to pull that into the deck.
Yeah, let's do that.
All right.
All right.
Well, thanks, everyone.
Have a good day.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on project status and align on the upcoming QBR agenda.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=175.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**QBR Scheduled:** The QBR is set for next week, extended by 30 mins to discuss strategic pivots.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot Proposed:** GrowthX proposed programmatic SEO to replace the slowing integrations workstream, prompting a review of a failed 2020 AI blog initiative.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
  - [**Critical QBR Inputs Needed:** Webflow must provide success metrics to guide the strategy, and both teams must deliver feedback on the Code Components POC doc.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=970.0)
  - [**AEO Tool Launching:** GrowthX's proprietary AEO visibility tool, "Check That," launches next week, enabling new analysis of Webflow's AI Overview performance.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

## Topics

### QBR Planning & Strategic Pivot

  - [**QBR Logistics:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**When:** Next week, same time + 30 mins.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**Attendees:** Jason confirmed; Colin will ask Rachel.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot Proposal:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [**Problem:** The integrations workstream is slowing as available integrations are exhausted.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1022.0)
      - [**Proposal:** Pivot to programmatic SEO to create targeted comparison pages (e.g., "Webflow vs. X").](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [**Rationale:** A narrow, targeted SEO play with a proven template from other GrowthX clients (e.g., Ramp).](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0)
  - [**Webflow's Historical Context:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=827.0)
      - [A similar AI-generated developer blog initiative in 2020 failed due to marketing team feedback (Luke Stahl) on quality.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=827.0)
      - [**QBR Focus:** GrowthX must explain what has changed in its process and modeling to ensure success this time.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)

### Current Project Status

  - [**Code Components POC:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [**Status:** Awaiting contract signature.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
      - [**Action:** Webflow to review the POC doc and provide feedback before the QBR.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
  - [**Integrations Workstream:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [**Icon API:** Zach is sourcing an API to simplify the workflow; a response is expected today.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [**Category Reordering:** Awaiting the green light to reorder categories.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [**Partner Comms Pipeline:** Progressing, despite challenges with sunset tools.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=322.0)
      - [**Publishing Capacity:** Resourced to "supercharge" publishing after a recent dip.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [**Integration Use Case Guides:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [**Status:** 16 guides are live.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [**Strategy Shift:** Pivoting from an SEO-first approach to a "principles-first" approach focused on user utility.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Avoids competing with Webflow's own content and aligns with Google's reward system for user-centric content.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)

### QBR Agenda & Required Inputs

  - [**QBR Agenda:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=422.0)
      - [Performance review of current workstreams.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Deep dive on the Code Components POC.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=970.0)
      - [Presentation of the programmatic SEO proposal.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [Discussion of the go-forward strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=988.0)
  - [**Required Inputs from Webflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)
      - [**Success Metrics:** Define how the marketing team measures success (e.g., "halo effect" on developer activity).](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)
      - [**AEO Traffic Analysis:** Help analyze integration page traffic, which appears "bot-like," to distinguish between good and bad bots.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
  - [**Required Inputs from GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**AEO Analysis:** Use the new "Check That" tool to analyze Webflow's AI Overview performance, focusing on "beginner developer" content.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [**Programmatic SEO Rationale:** Explain how the proposed process differs from the failed 2020 initiative.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Colin:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)
      - [Confirm Rachel's QBR attendance.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
      - [Provide success metrics for the QBR.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1071.0)
      - [Review the Code Components POC doc with the PM.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=251.0)
  - [**Zach:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [Provide an update on the icon API today.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
  - [**Liz:**](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=422.0)
      - [Prepare the QBR deck with the programmatic SEO proposal and AEO analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=674.0)
      - [Research the 2020 AI blog initiative to explain the process improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)
      - [Ticket an analysis of the "bot-like" traffic on integration pages.](https://fathom.video/share/oXURB5i5GXVR_YMK9BS-rBoAyoJx9fjT?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)

action_items.description: Share code components POC doc w/ PM; add feedback
Pull past call transcripts re: programmatic SEO; prep QBR answers
Provide feedback on Kirkland one-pager to GrowthX
Define and share success metrics/goals w/ Liz
Open ticket re: bot vs human traffic on integration pages
